title: 帷幕
summary:
Poirot informs Hastings that his life is nearing its end and that a clever individual known as X might attempt to hasten his death for personal benefit. Poirot reveals that he has made arrangements for Hastings to take over his role, leaving physical clues in a locked briefcase that will lead Hastings to the truth, even if that truth is difficult to face.

Following a pleasant dinner at Stile's Manor involving Mrs. Letreul, Dr. Franklin, and Nurse Crewe, the group plays cards. Hastings visits Poirot with Norton, who intends to lend him a book. Later, Hastings observes Boyd Carlington appearing preoccupied and uncomfortable by the carefree atmosphere, eventually leaving after hearing distant thunder. During the night, Hastings is awakened by a noise and sees Norton returning to his room in striped pajamas, notably locking his door. This action causes Hastings to feel unease, as he recalls the mysterious disappearance of Poirot's room key.

The next morning, Hastings finds Poirot looking exhausted and ill. Poirot reveals that Norton reported seeing two people, but he refuses to disclose their identities to Hastings immediately, stating he wants to remember the details first. Poirot claims the case is essentially over but remains melancholy. After breakfast, Hastings walks in the garden and encounters an energetic Boyd Carlington. They notice that while other windows at the manor are open following the storm, Norton's window remains shut. Concerned that Norton might be ill, they decide to check on him.
